title: What are MySQL window frame specifications (ROWS vs RANGE)?
description: Explain ROWS vs RANGE in window function frame clauses.
options: ROWS defines the frame by physical row count (ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING = 3 rows). RANGE defines by logical value range (RANGE BETWEEN 10 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW = all rows with values within 10 of current). ROWS is deterministic; RANGE groups ties together. Default: RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
correct_answer: ROWS defines the frame by physical row count (ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING = 3 rows). RANGE defines by logical value range (RANGE BETWEEN 10 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW = all rows with values within 10 of current). ROWS is deterministic; RANGE groups ties together. Default: RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW

title: Write SQL to find the running total and moving average of daily sales.
description: Use window functions for running calculations.
correct_answer:
SELECT 
    sale_date,
    daily_amount,
    SUM(daily_amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS running_total,
    AVG(daily_amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS moving_avg_7day,
    daily_amount - LAG(daily_amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date) AS day_over_day_change
FROM daily_sales
ORDER BY sale_date;

title: What is MySQL's InnoDB Cluster and how does it ensure high availability?
options: InnoDB Cluster combines Group Replication (multi-master/single-primary), MySQL Shell (admin), and MySQL Router (automatic routing). Group Replication uses Paxos-based consensus for transaction certification; provides automatic failover, conflict detection, and data consistency. Router redirects queries to available primary/secondaries transparently
correct_answer: InnoDB Cluster combines Group Replication (multi-master/single-primary), MySQL Shell (admin), and MySQL Router (automatic routing). Group Replication uses Paxos-based consensus for transaction certification; provides automatic failover, conflict detection, and data consistency. Router redirects queries to available primary/secondaries transparently

title: Write SQL to implement a gap-and-island detection query.
description: Find consecutive groups (islands) in sequential data with gaps.
correct_answer:
-- Find consecutive date ranges (islands) for each user's login activity
WITH numbered AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        login_date,
        login_date - INTERVAL ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY login_date) DAY AS grp
    FROM user_logins
)
SELECT 
    user_id,
    MIN(login_date) AS streak_start,
    MAX(login_date) AS streak_end,
    DATEDIFF(MAX(login_date), MIN(login_date)) + 1 AS streak_length
FROM numbered
GROUP BY user_id, grp
HAVING streak_length >= 3
ORDER BY user_id, streak_start;

title: What is the difference between EXPLAIN and EXPLAIN ANALYZE in MySQL 8.0?
options: EXPLAIN shows the estimated query execution plan without running the query. EXPLAIN ANALYZE (MySQL 8.0.18+) actually executes the query and shows real timing, row counts, and iterator-based execution details. ANALYZE output includes: actual time per iterator, rows examined vs estimated, number of loops. Critical for identifying where estimates diverge from reality
correct_answer: EXPLAIN shows the estimated query execution plan without running the query. EXPLAIN ANALYZE (MySQL 8.0.18+) actually executes the query and shows real timing, row counts, and iterator-based execution details. ANALYZE output includes: actual time per iterator, rows examined vs estimated, number of loops. Critical for identifying where estimates diverge from reality

title: What are MySQL histogram statistics?
description: How do histograms improve query optimization?
options: ANALYZE TABLE t UPDATE HISTOGRAM ON col creates a histogram of value distribution. Two types: singleton (one bucket per distinct value) for low-cardinality; equi-height (equal frequency buckets) for high-cardinality. Optimizer uses histograms to estimate selectivity of WHERE conditions more accurately than simple index statistics, especially for non-indexed columns
correct_answer: ANALYZE TABLE t UPDATE HISTOGRAM ON col creates a histogram of value distribution. Two types: singleton (one bucket per distinct value) for low-cardinality; equi-height (equal frequency buckets) for high-cardinality. Optimizer uses histograms to estimate selectivity of WHERE conditions more accurately than simple index statistics, especially for non-indexed columns

title: Write SQL to find customers with purchases in every product category.
description: Use relational division to find all-category customers.
correct_answer:
-- Relational division: customers who bought from ALL categories
SELECT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    -- Find any category where customer has NO purchase
    SELECT cat.id
    FROM categories cat
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM orders o
        JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id
        JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
        WHERE o.customer_id = c.id
        AND p.category_id = cat.id
    )
)
ORDER BY c.name;

-- Alternative using COUNT:
-- SELECT c.id, c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ... 
-- GROUP BY c.id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.category_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM categories)

title: What is MySQL's invisible column feature?
options: Invisible columns are excluded from SELECT * output but can still be explicitly queried, indexed, and used in WHERE/JOIN. Created with: ALTER TABLE t ADD COLUMN secret INT INVISIBLE. Useful for adding columns without breaking SELECT * dependent code, internal metadata, or soft-migration. Still visible in SHOW CREATE TABLE and information_schema
correct_answer: Invisible columns are excluded from SELECT * output but can still be explicitly queried, indexed, and used in WHERE/JOIN. Created with: ALTER TABLE t ADD COLUMN secret INT INVISIBLE. Useful for adding columns without breaking SELECT * dependent code, internal metadata, or soft-migration. Still visible in SHOW CREATE TABLE and information_schema

title: Write SQL to calculate year-over-year growth percentage by quarter.
correct_answer:
WITH quarterly_revenue AS (
    SELECT 
        YEAR(order_date) AS yr,
        QUARTER(order_date) AS qtr,
        SUM(total_amount) AS revenue
    FROM orders
    WHERE order_date >= '2022-01-01'
    GROUP BY YEAR(order_date), QUARTER(order_date)
)
SELECT 
    curr.yr,
    curr.qtr,
    curr.revenue AS current_revenue,
    prev.revenue AS prev_year_revenue,
    ROUND(((curr.revenue - prev.revenue) / prev.revenue) * 100, 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM quarterly_revenue curr
LEFT JOIN quarterly_revenue prev 
    ON curr.qtr = prev.qtr AND curr.yr = prev.yr + 1
ORDER BY curr.yr, curr.qtr;
